formula: Al2O3 (with Fe2+, Ti4+, V3+, Cr3+, etc.)
_all_text: Sapphire sapphire Al2O3 (with Fe2+, Ti4+, V3+, Cr3+, etc.) The color diversity of sapphire reflects the range of trace element chromophores: blue (Fe2+/Ti4+ IVCT), yellow (Fe3+), green (Fe2+), pink/purple (Cr3+/V3+), orange-pink padparadscha (Cr3++iron-related). Beryllium diffusion treatment in the 2000s allowed production of vibrant yellow and orange colors by inducing new color centers. Kashmir sapphire produces a 'velvety' blue that seems to glow from within — caused by fine silk inclusions scattering light. Star sapphire at its best shows a perfectly centered six-rayed star that moves over the cabochon surface. Padparadscha's salmon-pink-orange is unique and defies category — it must be evaluated in person to appreciate. Ancient Persians believed the sky was blue because the Earth rested on a vast sapphire pedestal whose reflection made the sky blue. Medieval European clergy wore sapphire rings as stones of heaven. The name comes from Greek 'sappheiros' (possibly originally referring to lapis lazuli, not sapphire). India's Vedic astrological tradition gives sapphire to Saturn (Shani) — 'Neelam' — with complex prescriptions for when wearing it is beneficial or harmful. The engagement ring tradition of sapphire received a 20th-century revival through the 1981 Prince Charles/Princess Diana ring. wisdom and clarity heaven and divine favor truth and fidelity royal power September and autumn Royal jewelry and regalia across all major civilizations Symbol of divine favor in medieval Christian tradition Set in papal rings and bishops' rings (sapphire as stone of heaven) Mughal jewelry (major collector) Princess Diana/Kate Middleton engagement ring — most famous sapphire in modern history Engraved sapphires as talismans Navigation (blue sapphire as 'guiding star' stone) blue sapphire Kashmir sapphire Ceylon sapphire star sapphire padparadscha September birthstone Al2O3 iron titanium corundum blue velvet sapphire oxide variety corundum gemstone blue birthstone september trigonal high-priority jewelry royal velvety-Kashmir-blue heaven-stone September-depth iron-titanium-charge-transfer royal-celestial blue cornflower-blue deep-blue
